source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.474 (scan idx 473), TAB XXVII
family: labyrinthus-radialis-magnus (giant concentric-ring wheel; centre MARIA medallion; Marian twin of TAB XXVI)
title_diplomatic: MARIA STELLA CELEBRATA LABYRINTHO CONTINENTE. Disticha retrograda 69,902,227,514,327,040 ad est versus simplices 279,608,910,057,308,160. Ideam [scansion] invenit I. Caramuel anno 1656. Voces addidit R.P. Nicolaus Lucensis Capucinus, Theologus, Concionator, Vir eruditissimus anno 1649. R.P. HIPPOLYTO MARRACCIO LUCENSI E CONGR. CLER. REGULARIUM MATRIS DEI PARTHENIARUM LAUDUM ENCOMIASTAE etc. Observantiae gratia Satrianenses Alumni consecrabant anno 1662.
title_translation: MARY THE STAR, celebrated by this enclosing labyrinth. Retrograde distichs 69,902,227,514,327,040 — that is, 279,608,910,057,308,160 simple verses. Design by Caramuel (1656), words by Nicolaus of Lucca (1649); consecrated to Fr. Hippolytus Marraccio, encomiast of the praises of the Virgin Mother of God, by the alumni of Satriano, 1662.

author_combinatorial_claim.note: Identical magnitude to TAB XXVI — the two form a Christ/Mary pair (IESVS SOL / MARIA STELLA), same design, same ~2.8e17 count. The retrograde-distich figure is now fully legible here: 69,902,227,514,327,040.

confidence: mixed (centre + numerals high, ring cells low)
calibration_role: Twin polar plate to XXVI. The Christ/Mary pairing with identical combinatorial design tests the ORIGIN-SIGNATURE engine: two plates by the same generator (Caramuel design + Lucensis words) should produce near-identical ambiguity signatures (low JSD) — a built-in same-author validation set.
